title: Parser: reject malformed sensor frames with a typed error
body:
## What to build

When the frame decoder receives a payload whose checksum does not match, it returns a typed `FrameError` instead of raising or silently dropping the frame, so callers can distinguish a corrupt frame from an empty read. Pure software: exercised entirely off-target against captured byte buffers.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A frame with a bad checksum yields a `FrameError` carrying the offending offset
- [ ] A well-formed frame still decodes to the expected struct
- [ ] The decoder never raises on malformed input
- [ ] Behavior is covered by fixture-driven host tests

Depends on: None
